hypothesis: If the six operating parameters (bicarbonate temperature, bicarbonate concentration, two flow rates, KOH concentration, and total applied current density) are optimized by a closed-loop Bayesian optimization driven by in-line mass spectrometry feedback during 5-minute electrolysis runs on silver nanoparticle–coated gas-diffusion electrodes, then the maximum reactor yield will be higher than that obtained from a random experimental design because the optimization efficiently identifies high-yield regions in the parameter space.
